rootCause: ASP.NET Core/Kestrel default request body limit was 30000000 bytes, below the 200 MiB WorkVideoPolicy maximum and below nginx 250m.
notChanged: ffmpeg/HLS transcoding behavior was not changed
public load-test target selection was not changed

validations.name: RED focused startup upload limit test
validations.result: failed before implementation with Kestrel request limit 30000000

validations.name: GREEN focused startup upload limit test
dev compose backend rebuild and health
validations.command: BACKEND_PUBLISH_PORT=18080 docker compose -f docker-compose.dev.yml up -d --build backend; curl http://127.0.0.1:18080/api/health

validations.name: live 35 MiB HLS upload-limit repro after rebuild
validations.command: authenticated curl multipart POST to /api/admin/works/{id}/videos/hls-job through http://127.0.0.1:3000
validations.result: changed from HTTP 413 Kestrel request body limit to HTTP 400 app-level MP4 validation

validations.name: backend solution tests
validations.result: passed: Contract 1 skipped, Component 126, Unit 59, Architecture 35, Integration 230

risks: A separate valid large MP4 transcode test is still needed if uploads fail after this fix.
The request body limit is global for the API; app-level validation and nginx still constrain video uploads.
Current working tree also includes runtime-pool-diagnostics WIP, so staging must be scoped.
recommendation: Ship this fix before continuing performance work if production HLS uploads larger than about 28.6 MiB are blocked.
